---
title: "CGIAR Climate Data Hub - System-Wide Climate Data Asset Mapping"
subtitle: "Internal review draft"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: false
    embed-resources: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        #TOC {
          position: sticky;
          top: 1rem;
          max-height: calc(100vh - 2rem);
          overflow-y: auto;
        }
        @media print {
          details.code-fold summary,
          details.code-fold .sourceCode,
          details.code-fold .cell-output {
            display: none !important;
          }
        }
        /* Tables: smaller type, tight padding, columns sized to content so the
           last (definition / rationale) column gets the remaining width. */
        main table, .cell table, table {
          font-size: 0.80rem;
          width: 100%;
          table-layout: auto;
          border-collapse: collapse;
        }
        main table th, main table td,
        table th, table td {
          padding: 0.28rem 0.5rem;
          vertical-align: top;
          line-height: 1.3;
        }
        /* Inline code (file paths) in tables must not wrap mid-path. */
        table code { white-space: nowrap; }
        /* Figure captions: tighter, smaller, secondary to the plot. */
        figure figcaption, .figure-caption, p > img + em {
          font-size: 0.82rem;
          color: #444;
          line-height: 1.35;
        }
        </style>
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{pdflscape}
  docx:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
# This document is a reproducible notebook: load the normalised data and compute
# all statistics once, then generate every section, figure, and table from them.
import pathlib
import sys
import re
import random
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from IPython.display import Markdown, display
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from itables import init_notebook_mode, show

sys.path.insert(0, str(pathlib.Path.cwd() / "src"))
from report_common import build_report_body, compute_stats, load

assets, merge_log = load()
stats = compute_stats(assets, merge_log)

# ---- Shared report styling -------------------------------------------------
INK, GREEN, BLUE, BLUE_LT = "#0F1F2B", "#033529", "#1955A6", "#63A9FE"
ACCESS_COLOUR = {"Open": "#1F8A70", "Restricted": "#C2691C", "Unknown": "#7C8A99"}
SEG_PALETTE = ["#1955A6", "#1F8A70", "#E0A11B", "#63A9FE", "#C2691C", "#17B89B",
               "#7D8CC4", "#8B5CF6", "#2E7D52", "#EC4899", "#5C6B73", "#AE6C7A",
               "#4A90D9", "#F4B942"]
FONT = "IBM Plex Sans, Source Sans 3, system-ui, sans-serif"

def style(fig, height=460, **kw):
    fig.update_layout(template="simple_white", height=height,
                      font=dict(family=FONT, size=12, color=INK),
                      title_font=dict(family=FONT, color=GREEN),
                      paper_bgcolor="white", plot_bgcolor="white",
                      margin=dict(l=70, r=24, t=34, b=52), **kw)
    return fig

def _link(a):
    nm = a.get("name", "")
    return f'<a href="{a["url"]}" target="_blank">{nm}</a>' if a.get("url") else nm

def _why(a, n=300):
    return (a.get("justification") or a.get("primary_use_case") or a.get("short_description") or "")[:n]

def itab(df, page=100, widths=None):
    # Fixed layout + explicit per-column widths so short columns (Rank, Access)
    # stay narrow and the long text column gets the space — no wasted whitespace,
    # no horizontal scroll. `widths` is a list of CSS widths, one per column.
    cdefs = [{"width": w, "targets": i} for i, w in enumerate(widths)] if widths else None
    show(df, classes="display compact", column_filters="header",
         paging=len(df) > page, lengthMenu=[page, 25, 50],
         scrollX=False, autoWidth=False, style="width:100%; table-layout:fixed;",
         columnDefs=cdefs, maxBytes=0, showIndex=False)

# ---- Tooltip enrichment helpers --------------------------------------------
# Tooltips should ADD what isn't already visible on the chart (which centres,
# how much is open, example assets), not repeat the printed count.
TOTAL = len(assets)

def _topc(sub, k=3):
    c = Counter(a["centre"].replace("_", "-") for a in sub)
    return ", ".join(f"{n} ({v})" for n, v in c.most_common(k)) or "—"

def _topd(sub, k=3):
    c = Counter(a["domain_norm"] for a in sub if a["domain_norm"] != "Not specified")
    return ", ".join(f"{n} ({v})" for n, v in c.most_common(k)) or "—"

def _openpct(sub):
    return round(100 * sum(1 for a in sub if a.get("access_norm") == "Open") / len(sub)) if sub else 0

def _eg(sub, k=2):
    return "; ".join((a.get("name") or "")[:36] for a in sub[:k]) or "—"

def hbar(cats, vals, hover, color=BLUE, xtitle="Number of assets"):
    f = go.Figure(go.Bar(
        x=vals, y=cats, orientation="h", marker_color=color, customdata=hover,
        hovertemplate="%{customdata}<extra></extra>", text=vals,
        textposition="auto", cliponaxis=False))
    style(f, height=max(250, 34 * len(cats) + 110),
          xaxis=dict(title=xtitle), yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"))
    return f

# Generate the narrative once, then split at the figure/table markers so the
# interactive figures and tables below are injected at the right positions.
body = build_report_body(assets, stats, figures_prefix="outputs/figures")
seg = re.split(r"<!--(?:FIG1|FIG2|FIG5|FIG3|FIG4|FIG6|TABLE1|QUADRANT|FIG8|TABLE2|ANNEXA)-->", body)
seg += [""] * (12 - len(seg))   # guard if a marker is ever missing

# Tables built once; rendered interactively (itables) in HTML and as static
# Markdown tables in Word/PDF.
def _nm(a):
    return (a.get("name") or "")

df_strategic = pd.DataFrame([{
    "Centre": a["centre"].replace("_", "-"), "Rank": a.get("asset_rank") or "—",
    "Asset": _nm(a), "Domain": a["domain_norm"], "Access": a["access_norm"],
    "Justification": _why(a, 240)} for a in stats["strategic"]])
df_actnow = pd.DataFrame([{
    "Centre": a["centre"].replace("_", "-"), "Asset": _nm(a), "Domain": a["domain_norm"],
    "Score": a.get("priority_score"), "Pathway": a.get("integration_hint", ""),
    "Rationale": _why(a, 240)} for a in stats["ingest_now"]])
df_annexa = pd.DataFrame([{
    "Centre": a["centre"].replace("_", "-"), "Asset": _nm(a), "Domain": a["domain_norm"],
    "Geography": a["geo_norm"], "Type": a.get("type_norm", ""),
    "Score": a.get("priority_score"), "Access": a.get("access_norm", ""),
    "Pathway": a.get("integration_hint", "")}
    for a in sorted(assets, key=lambda x: -(x.get("priority_score") or 0))])

def md_table(df):
    display(Markdown(df.to_markdown(index=False)))

import textwrap

def grid_table(df, charw):
    # Emit a pandoc GRID table with fixed per-column character widths. Unlike
    # pipe tables, grid tables carry column widths in the AST, so the Word AND
    # PDF writers honour them (short columns stay narrow, text columns wrap
    # within their width) — no autofit mangling.
    cols = list(df.columns)
    def lines_for(text, w):
        text = str("" if text is None else text).replace("\n", " ").strip()
        return textwrap.wrap(text, w) or [""]
    def rule(ch):
        return "+" + "+".join(ch * (w + 2) for w in charw) + "+"
    def row(cells):
        wrapped = [lines_for(cells[i], charw[i]) for i in range(len(cols))]
        h = max(len(x) for x in wrapped)
        out = []
        for li in range(h):
            out.append("| " + " | ".join(
                (wrapped[i][li] if li < len(wrapped[i]) else "").ljust(charw[i])
                for i in range(len(cols))) + " |")
        return out
    lines = [rule("-")] + row(list(cols)) + [rule("=")]
    for _, r in df.iterrows():
        lines += row([r[c] for c in cols]) + [rule("-")]
    display(Markdown("\n".join(lines)))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# itables interactive mode — HTML only (its injected HTML must not leak into
# the PDF/Word output as a stray "<IPython ... HTML object>").
init_notebook_mode(all_interactive=True)
```
:::

**Version 1.0.1 (draft)** — `{python} stats["total"]` assets in this build. **Interactive data access:** [CDH interactive dashboard](docs/index.html).

> Generated programmatically from the data — no numbers are hand-typed. The figures and tables
> below are interactive (hover, sort, filter) in this HTML edition. See **Data Access, Feedback
> and Reproducibility** near the end for the source code, steps to reproduce, and how to suggest
> corrections or additions.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[0]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# Figure 1 — assets per centre, split into one segment per nominator.
# Tooltip adds WHO contributed (not visible from the bar alone).
by_centre = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(int))
totals = Counter()
for a in assets:
    nm = (a.get("nominator") or "Unattributed").split("\n")[0].strip() or "Unattributed"
    by_centre[a["centre"]][nm] += 1
    totals[a["centre"]] += 1
centres = sorted(totals, key=totals.get)
f1 = go.Figure()
for c in centres:
    base = 0
    for i, (nm, cnt) in enumerate(sorted(by_centre[c].items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])):
        f1.add_bar(y=[c.replace("_", "-")], x=[cnt], base=base, orientation="h",
                   width=0.7, marker_color=SEG_PALETTE[i % len(SEG_PALETTE)],
                   showlegend=False,
                   hovertemplate=f"<b>{nm}</b>: {cnt} asset(s)<extra></extra>")
        base += cnt
    f1.add_annotation(x=base, y=c.replace("_", "-"), text=str(base), showarrow=False,
                      xshift=11, font=dict(size=11, color=INK))
style(f1, barmode="overlay",
      xaxis=dict(title="Number of assets (bar split by nominator)"),
      yaxis=dict(categoryorder="array",
                 categoryarray=[c.replace("_", "-") for c in centres]))
f1
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
![](outputs/figures/fig1_assets_per_centre.png)
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[1]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# Figure 2 — domain distribution. Tooltip adds % of portfolio, contributing
# centres, and open-access share.
_doms = [d for d, _ in Counter(a["domain_norm"] for a in assets
                               if a["domain_norm"] != "Not specified").most_common()]
_v, _h = [], []
for d in _doms:
    sub = [a for a in assets if a["domain_norm"] == d]
    _v.append(len(sub))
    _h.append(f"<b>{d}</b><br>{len(sub)} assets · {round(100*len(sub)/TOTAL)}% of portfolio"
              f"<br>Centres: {_topc(sub)}<br>{_openpct(sub)}% open access")
hbar(_doms, _v, _h)
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
![](outputs/figures/fig2_climate_domains.png)
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[2]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# Figure 5 — centre × domain. Tooltip adds example assets and open share.
HD = ["Adaptation Analytics", "Hazard", "Multi-domain", "Exposure", "Mitigation",
      "Hazard / Climate Services", "Sensitivity", "Adaptive Capacity"]
HD_SHORT = ["Adapt.<br>Analytics", "Hazard", "Multi-<br>domain", "Exposure", "Mitigation",
            "Hazard /<br>Clim. Svc", "Sensitivity", "Adaptive<br>Cap."]
CO = ["AfricaRice", "Alliance", "CIFOR_ICRAF", "CIP", "ICRISAT", "IFPRI",
      "IITA", "ILRI", "IRRI", "IWMI", "WorldFish"]
z5 = [[0] * len(HD) for _ in CO]
cd5 = [[""] * len(HD) for _ in CO]
for ri, c in enumerate(CO):
    for ci, d in enumerate(HD):
        sub = [a for a in assets if a["centre"] == c and a["domain_norm"] == d]
        z5[ri][ci] = len(sub)
        cd5[ri][ci] = (f"{len(sub)} asset(s) · {_openpct(sub)}% open<br>e.g. {_eg(sub)}"
                       if sub else "none in this centre")
f5 = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
    z=z5, x=HD_SHORT, y=[c.replace("_", "-") for c in CO], xgap=3, ygap=3, showscale=False,
    colorscale=[[0, "#FFFFFF"], [0.18, "#DCEBFB"], [0.55, BLUE_LT], [1, BLUE]],
    customdata=cd5, text=[[str(v) if v else "" for v in row] for row in z5],
    texttemplate="%{text}", textfont=dict(size=10),
    hovertemplate="<b>%{y} × %{x}</b><br>%{customdata}<extra></extra>"))
style(f5, height=470, xaxis=dict(side="top", tickfont=dict(size=9)),
      yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"))
f5
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
![](outputs/figures/fig5_heatmap_centre_domain.png)
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[3]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# Figure 3 — asset type (ownership). Tooltip adds % share, centres, open share.
_types = [t for t, _ in Counter(a["type_norm"] for a in assets if a.get("type_norm")).most_common()]
_v, _h = [], []
for t in _types:
    sub = [a for a in assets if a.get("type_norm") == t]
    _v.append(len(sub))
    _h.append(f"<b>{t}</b><br>{len(sub)} assets · {round(100*len(sub)/TOTAL)}% of portfolio"
              f"<br>Centres: {_topc(sub)}<br>{_openpct(sub)}% open access")
hbar(_types, _v, _h)
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
![](outputs/figures/fig3_asset_types.png)
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[4]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# Figure 4 — geographic coverage. Tooltip adds dominant domains and centres.
GEO_ORDER = ["Africa", "Global", "Asia / South & SE Asia",
             "Latin America & Caribbean", "Multi-regional"]
_geos = [g for g in GEO_ORDER if any(a["geo_norm"] == g for a in assets)]
_v, _h = [], []
for g in _geos:
    sub = [a for a in assets if a["geo_norm"] == g]
    _v.append(len(sub))
    _h.append(f"<b>{g}</b><br>{len(sub)} assets · {round(100*len(sub)/TOTAL)}% of portfolio"
              f"<br>Top domains: {_topd(sub)}<br>Centres: {_topc(sub)}")
hbar(_geos, _v, _h)
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
![](outputs/figures/fig4_geographic_coverage.png)
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[5]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# Figure 6 — domain × geography. Tooltip adds the centres behind each cell and
# its open-access share; red dot marks a true coverage gap.
DOMAINS = ["Hazard", "Hazard / Climate Services", "Exposure", "Sensitivity",
           "Adaptive Capacity", "Adaptation Analytics", "Mitigation",
           "Climate Policy / Finance", "Multi-domain"]
GEOS = ["Africa", "Asia / South & SE Asia", "Latin America & Caribbean",
        "Global", "Multi-regional"]
GEO_SHORT = ["Africa", "Asia /<br>SE Asia", "Lat. Am. /<br>Caribbean",
             "Global", "Multi-<br>regional"]
M = [[0] * len(GEOS) for _ in DOMAINS]
CD = [[""] * len(GEOS) for _ in DOMAINS]
for ri, d in enumerate(DOMAINS):
    for ci, g in enumerate(GEOS):
        sub = [a for a in assets if a["domain_norm"] == d and a["geo_norm"] == g]
        M[ri][ci] = len(sub)
        CD[ri][ci] = ("no assets — coverage gap" if not sub
                      else f"{len(sub)} asset(s) · {_openpct(sub)}% open<br>Centres: {_topc(sub)}")
f6 = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
    z=M, x=GEO_SHORT, y=DOMAINS, xgap=3, ygap=3, showscale=False,
    colorscale=[[0, "#FFFFFF"], [0.18, "#DCEBFB"], [0.55, BLUE_LT], [1, BLUE]],
    customdata=CD, text=[[str(v) if v else "" for v in row] for row in M],
    texttemplate="%{text}", textfont=dict(size=11),
    hovertemplate="<b>%{y} × %{x}</b><br>%{customdata}<extra></extra>"))
for ri, row in enumerate(M):
    for ci, v in enumerate(row):
        if v == 0:
            f6.add_annotation(x=GEO_SHORT[ci], y=DOMAINS[ri], text="•",
                              showarrow=False, font=dict(color="#D98273", size=13))
style(f6, height=440, xaxis=dict(side="top", tickfont=dict(size=10)),
      yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"))
f6
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
![](outputs/figures/fig6_gap_matrix.png)
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[6]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
itab(df_strategic, widths=["9%", "5%", "28%", "15%", "10%", "33%"])
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
```{=latex}
\begin{landscape}\footnotesize
```
```{=openxml}
<w:p><w:pPr><w:sectPr><w:type w:val="nextPage"/><w:pgSz w:w="12240" w:h="15840"/></w:sectPr></w:pPr></w:p>
```
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
grid_table(df_strategic, [11, 4, 30, 15, 10, 46])
```
```{=openxml}
<w:p><w:pPr><w:sectPr><w:pgSz w:w="15840" w:h="12240" w:orient="landscape"/><w:pgMar w:top="720" w:bottom="720" w:left="720" w:right="720"/></w:sectPr></w:pPr></w:p>
```
```{=latex}
\end{landscape}
```
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[7]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# Figure 7 — readiness × reuse; colour = access, hover identifies the asset.
_jit = random.Random(42)
qfig = go.Figure()
for _acc, _col in ACCESS_COLOUR.items():
    _pts = [a for a in assets if a.get("access_norm") == _acc
            and a.get("score_components", {}).get("technical_readiness") is not None
            and a.get("score_components", {}).get("reuse_potential") is not None]
    qfig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=[a["score_components"]["technical_readiness"] + _jit.gauss(0, 0.018) for a in _pts],
        y=[a["score_components"]["reuse_potential"] + _jit.gauss(0, 0.018) for a in _pts],
        mode="markers", name=_acc,
        marker=dict(size=11, color=_col, opacity=0.72, line=dict(width=1, color="white")),
        customdata=[[a["name"], a["centre"], a["domain_norm"], _acc,
                     a.get("priority_score", "—")] for a in _pts],
        hovertemplate=("<b>%{customdata[0]}</b><br>%{customdata[1]} · %{customdata[2]}"
                       "<br>Access: %{customdata[3]} · Priority: %{customdata[4]}<extra></extra>"),
    ))
qfig.add_shape(type="rect", x0=0.7, y0=0.7, x1=1.06, y1=1.06,
               fillcolor="rgba(31,138,112,0.20)", line=dict(color="#1F8A70", width=1, dash="dot"),
               layer="below")
qfig.add_annotation(x=1.04, y=1.04, text="<b>Quick wins</b>", showarrow=False,
                    xanchor="right", font=dict(color="#1F8A70", size=12))
style(qfig, height=480, legend_title_text="Access",
      xaxis=dict(title="Technical readiness →", range=[0.15, 1.08]),
      yaxis=dict(title="Reuse potential →", range=[0.15, 1.08]))
qfig
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
![](outputs/figures/fig7_priority_quadrant.png)
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[8]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
# Figure 8 — integration pathway. Tooltip adds the file formats driving each
# pathway and example assets.
PORDER = ["Federate — ready", "Federate or light ingest", "Ingest candidate",
          "Negotiate access", "Assess"]
PCOL = {"Federate — ready": "#2E7D52", "Federate or light ingest": "#0E9E83",
        "Ingest candidate": BLUE_LT, "Negotiate access": "#C2691C", "Assess": "#7C8A99"}
_paths = [p for p in PORDER if any(a.get("integration_hint") == p for a in assets)]
_v, _h, _c = [], [], []
for p in _paths:
    sub = [a for a in assets if a.get("integration_hint") == p]
    fmts = Counter((a.get("file_format") or "—").split(",")[0].strip()[:20] for a in sub)
    _v.append(len(sub))
    _c.append(PCOL[p])
    _h.append(f"<b>{p}</b><br>{len(sub)} assets · {round(100*len(sub)/TOTAL)}% of portfolio"
              f"<br>Formats: {', '.join(n for n, _ in fmts.most_common(3))}<br>e.g. {_eg(sub)}")
hbar(_paths, _v, _h, color=_c)
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
![](outputs/figures/fig8_integration_pathway.png)
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[9]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
itab(df_actnow, widths=["9%", "27%", "15%", "6%", "14%", "29%"])
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
```{=latex}
\begin{landscape}\footnotesize
```
```{=openxml}
<w:p><w:pPr><w:sectPr><w:type w:val="nextPage"/><w:pgSz w:w="12240" w:h="15840"/></w:sectPr></w:pPr></w:p>
```
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
grid_table(df_actnow, [11, 30, 15, 6, 14, 40])
```
```{=openxml}
<w:p><w:pPr><w:sectPr><w:pgSz w:w="15840" w:h="12240" w:orient="landscape"/><w:pgMar w:top="720" w:bottom="720" w:left="720" w:right="720"/></w:sectPr></w:pPr></w:p>
```
```{=latex}
\end{landscape}
```
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[10]))
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{python}
#| echo: false
itab(df_annexa, widths=["9%", "25%", "13%", "12%", "11%", "6%", "9%", "15%"])
```
:::
::: {.content-visible unless-format="html"}
```{=latex}
\begin{landscape}\scriptsize
```
```{=openxml}
<w:p><w:pPr><w:sectPr><w:type w:val="nextPage"/><w:pgSz w:w="12240" w:h="15840"/></w:sectPr></w:pPr></w:p>
```
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
grid_table(df_annexa, [10, 30, 14, 13, 12, 5, 10, 14])
```
```{=openxml}
<w:p><w:pPr><w:sectPr><w:pgSz w:w="15840" w:h="12240" w:orient="landscape"/><w:pgMar w:top="720" w:bottom="720" w:left="720" w:right="720"/></w:sectPr></w:pPr></w:p>
```
```{=latex}
\end{landscape}
```
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[11]))
```
